Tool_senior_citizen_tax_Description: Higher basic exemption for 60+/80+ and Section 87A rebate — compare vs regular taxpayer (educational).
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_LearnText: Seniors (60+) get a ₹3 lakh basic exemption and super-seniors (80+) ₹5 lakh under the old-regime framing. Section 87A can wipe out tax if income is within the rebate limit. Compare your tax with a regular taxpayer on the same income.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_FormulaNote: Simplified old-regime style slabs and 87A (income ≤ ₹5L). New-regime 87A up to ₹12L is not modelled here. Educational only.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_VerdictNote: Estimated tax {0}. Higher exemption / rebate path saves about {1} vs a regular taxpayer (illustrative).
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: Same income as regular, senior, and super-senior
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_ExampleStep_4: Income ≤ ₹5L → 87A rebate up to ₹12,500 → tax after rebate = ₹0.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_ExampleStep_5: Compare with regular (₹2.5L exemption) in the scenario cards.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_Step_3: Apply higher exemption and slab tax.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_Step_4: Apply 87A if income ≤ ₹5L; compare vs regular.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_Info_Purpose: Estimate how senior / super-senior basic exemption and 87A change tax vs a regular taxpayer.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Age-based exemption and rebate can zero out tax that a younger person would still pay.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_Info_RealLife: Pension income planning, Form 16 checks, choosing old vs new regime with a CA.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_Info_Alt_Tax_Whe: For full regime and deduction modelling.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_Info_Alt_Scss_Wh: For senior citizen savings scheme returns.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_Info_Differentia: FinYatra highlights senior vs regular exemption and 87A side by side.
Tool_senior_citizen_tax_Info_Motto: Age can change your tax bill — check the exemption first.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_Description: At exit: lump up to 60% (tax-free) + annuity on the rest — see amounts and illustrative pension.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_LearnText: On normal NPS exit, you may take up to 60% as a tax-free lump sum; the rest buys an annuity. Enter corpus and lump %, see annuity corpus and an illustrative monthly pension.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_FormulaNote: Educational. Annuity rates vary by insurer and option. Early exit and tax rules differ — confirm with your CRA / CA.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_LumpHint: At normal exit, lump up to 60% is typically tax-free.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_Step_4: Estimate pension at assumed annuity rate.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_Info_Purpose: Split NPS corpus into lump (max 60%) and annuity, with an illustrative pension.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_Info_WhyKnow: The 40% annuity rule shapes retirement cash flow — plan the split before exit.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_Info_RealLife: Retirement exit planning, comparing annuity options, tax-free lump budgeting.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_Info_Alt_Nps_When: To project corpus while still contributing.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_Info_Alt_Tax_When: For tax on annuity income in retirement.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows lump, annuity corpus, and illustrative pension in one exit view.
Tool_nps_withdrawal_Info_Motto: Plan the 60/40 split before you exit NPS.
Tool_pm_kisan_Description: ₹6,000/year in 3 instalments of ₹2,000 — eligibility display for PM-Kisan Samman Nidhi.
Tool_pm_kisan_LearnText: Eligible landholding farmer families receive ₹6,000 per year under PM-Kisan, paid in three instalments of ₹2,000. Toggle eligibility to see the benefit schedule.
Tool_pm_kisan_FormulaNote: Educational display only. Actual eligibility, e-KYC, and payment status are on the official PM-Kisan portal.
Tool_pm_kisan_EligibleHint: Simplified flag. Confirm landholding and exclusion criteria on the official portal.
Tool_pm_kisan_VerdictNo: Not marked eligible — annual benefit shows ₹0. Check the official PM-Kisan portal for status.
Tool_pm_kisan_ExampleStep_4: Three instalments display in the schedule.
Tool_pm_kisan_ExampleStep_5: If not eligible, all amounts show ₹0.
Tool_pm_kisan_Step_4: Confirm status on the official portal.
Tool_pm_kisan_Info_Purpose: Show the ₹6,000 annual PM-Kisan benefit in three instalments when eligible.
Tool_pm_kisan_Info_WhyKnow: Knowing the instalment schedule helps plan farm cash flow.
Tool_pm_kisan_Info_RealLife: Checking expected instalments after e-KYC, budgeting seasonal expenses.
Tool_pm_kisan_Info_Alt_Budget_When: To fit instalments into household cash flow.
Tool_pm_kisan_Info_Alt_Goal_When: Saving instalments toward a farm goal.
Tool_pm_kisan_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows the three ₹2,000 instalments clearly when eligible.
Tool_pm_kisan_Info_Motto: ₹6,000 in three parts — know your instalment calendar.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_Description: Simplified PMAY interest subsidy by EWS/LIG/MIG category — estimate on eligible loan amount.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_LearnText: PMAY CLSS-style interest subsidy depends on income category: EWS/LIG get a higher rate on loans up to ₹6 lakh; MIG gets lower rates on higher caps. Enter loan and category for a simplified subsidy estimate.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_FormulaNote: Illustrative CLSS-style rates. PMAY Urban 2.0 rules may differ. Confirm with your lender / PMAY portal.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_CategoryHint: EWS/LIG: 6.5% on ≤₹6L · MIG-I: 4% on ≤₹9L · MIG-II: 3% on ≤₹12L.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_VerdictNote: Estimated interest subsidy {0} on subsidised loan {1} at {2}% (illustrative).
Tool_pmay_subsidy_ScenarioSubtitle: Same loan under EWS, MIG-I, MIG-II
Tool_pmay_subsidy_ExampleStep_4: Subsidy ≈ PV of 6.5% interest differential on ₹6L over 20 years.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_ExampleStep_5: Compare EWS vs MIG rates in scenario cards.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_Step_2: Choose EWS, LIG, MIG-I, or MIG-II.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_Info_Purpose: Estimate CLSS-style interest subsidy by EWS/LIG/MIG category.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_Info_WhyKnow: Category and loan cap decide how much interest support you may get.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_Info_RealLife: Home loan planning under PMAY, comparing EWS vs MIG eligibility.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra maps EWS/LIG/MIG caps and rates into one subsidy estimate.
Tool_pmay_subsidy_Info_Motto: Category and cap decide your PMAY interest support.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Description: Same total invested via monthly SIP vs one-time lumpsum — compare future values at your return rate.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Subtitle: Same money in · SIP FV vs lumpsum FV
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_LearnText: Invest the same total either as a monthly SIP or as a lumpsum on day one. Lumpsum usually grows more if returns are steady (money works longer); SIP spreads risk over time. Compare both FVs here.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_FormulaNote: Assumes constant return. Real markets are volatile — SIP can help with rupee-cost averaging. Educational only.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_VerdictNote: SIP FV {0} · lumpsum FV {1} · gap {2} (same total invested).
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_ExampleStep_3: SIP FV uses monthly compounding formula.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_ExampleStep_5: Lumpsum is usually higher if returns are constant — SIP still helps with timing risk.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Step_1: Enter monthly SIP, years, and return.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Step_3: Compute SIP FV and lumpsum FV of that total.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Step_4: Compare the difference and rate scenarios.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Info_Purpose: Compare future value when the same total is invested via SIP vs lumpsum.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Info_WhyKnow: Lumpsum wins on paper with steady returns; SIP wins on discipline and averaging.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Info_RealLife: Bonus vs SIP decision, deploying a windfall, equity allocation timing.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Info_Goal_2: See the gap at your return rate
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Info_Alt_Mf_When: For mode toggle SIP/lumpsum on one fund.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra locks the same total invested and shows both FVs side by side.
Tool_sip_vs_lumpsum_Info_Motto: Same rupees in — see which path grows more on paper.
Tool_home_insurance_Description: Estimate home insurance premium from property value and rate — structure cover educational estimate.
Tool_home_insurance_LearnText: Home insurance premiums are often a small percentage of the sum insured (rebuilding cost / property value). Enter value and rate to estimate annual premium. Add-ons (earthquake, contents) are not modelled here.
Tool_home_insurance_FormulaNote: Illustrative only. Real quotes depend on construction, location, age, and add-ons. GST may apply on premium.
Tool_home_insurance_PropertyHint: Often rebuilding cost, not market land value.
Tool_home_insurance_RateHint: Typical structure rates are often around 0.05–0.2% — check insurer quotes.
Tool_home_insurance_VerdictNote: Estimated annual premium {0} for sum insured {1} (illustrative).
Tool_home_insurance_ExampleStep_5: Ask insurers for structure + contents quotes with GST.
Tool_home_insurance_Step_4: Compare low vs high rate scenarios.
Tool_home_insurance_Info_Purpose: Estimate annual home insurance premium from property value and rate.
Tool_home_insurance_Info_WhyKnow: A small rate on a large sum insured still matters in the household budget.
Tool_home_insurance_Info_RealLife: New home cover, renewing structure policy, comparing insurer rates.
Tool_home_insurance_Info_Alt_Health_When: For medical cover need, not property.
Tool_home_insurance_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra gives a clear value × rate premium with rate scenarios.
Tool_home_insurance_Info_Motto: Insure the rebuild cost — not just the market price.
